footer.tagline: One design system, every project in sync.

landing.heroBadge: New: Flex · Grid · Table · v0.1.8 on npm
landing.heroSubtitle: A React 19 + Tailwind v4 design system: token-driven, three themes, a framework-agnostic adapter layer. Edit once, version it, and sync every project you ship.

quickStart.title: See it in code
quickStart.subtitle: Install the package, import a component, theme it with tokens. The same CodeBlock you see here is a YunUI pattern.

why.title: One source of truth
why.subtitle: A component library built to be the single source of truth across every project you ship.
why.componentsDesc: Primitives, page-level patterns, and AI product components — composed, typed, and ready to ship.
why.themesDesc: Token-driven theming. Light, zinc-dark, and a true-black OLED theme — swap with one class.
why.adaptersDesc: Bring your own Link, Image, and i18n. Inject them once; the library stays portable across apps.
why.releasesDesc: Published to npm via trusted publishing with provenance — no long-lived tokens in CI.
why.testsDesc: Strict TypeScript, JSDoc on the public surface, and a test suite that guards every primitive.
why.productionDesc: Battle-tested in production — already powering a real, shipping app end to end.

livePreview.title: Theming, live
livePreview.subtitle: Real YunUI components — flip the theme switch (top-right) to see light, zinc-dark, and true-black update in place.
livePreview.proPlanDesc: Everything in Basic, plus higher limits and priority support.

highlights.title: A taste of the kit
highlights.subtitle: A handful of flagship pieces — see the full set in the showcase.
highlights.cardDesc: A Card primitive — everything in Basic, plus higher limits.

faq.title: Questions, answered
faq.subtitle: The things people ask before adopting YunUI in a real project.
faq.a1: Everything is driven by CSS custom-property tokens. Import the stylesheet, then toggle a theme class (light / dark / true-black) on the root html element. Override any token in your own CSS and every component follows — no prop drilling, no theme objects to thread through the tree.
faq.q2: Does it need a specific framework?
faq.a2: No. The core primitives are framework-agnostic. Routing, image, and i18n concerns are injected through a thin adapter layer (YunUIProvider) — wire in Next's Link/Image or your own, and the library stays portable across apps.
faq.a3: Edit once in the library, version it, and sync every consuming project via npm. Releases are published with provenance through tokenless OIDC trusted publishing — no long-lived secrets in CI.
faq.a4: Interactive primitives build on Radix-style behaviors with keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA wired in. Components ship typed and JSDoc'd, and a 141-case test suite guards the public surface.

cta.title: One design system, every project in sync.
cta.subtitle: Browse the gallery, read the docs, or jump straight into the source on GitHub.

showcase.title: Every YunUI component, on one page.
showcase.intro: The full gallery — every exported primitive, form control, overlay, data-display piece, layout chrome, pattern and AI component, each in a live Preview / Code frame. Jump to a category below or scroll the whole thing.

foundations.title: Foundations
foundations.description: The tokens, type scale, surfaces and motion that define the YunUI look.

design.title: Utility Classes
design.description: Yunxin pages often use these classes directly — the design lives in CSS, components are thin wrappers.
